item.title: PolicyDec 18, 2025Project Vend: Phase twoIn June, we revealed that we’d set up a small shop in our San Francisco office lunchroom, run by an AI shopkeeper. It was part of Project Vend, a free-form experiment exploring how well AIs could do on complex, real-world tasks. How has Claude's business been since we last wrote?
item.description: PolicyDec 18, 2025Project Vend: Phase twoIn June, we revealed that we’d set up a small shop in our San Francisco office lunchroom, run by an AI shopkeeper. It was part of Project Vend, a free-form experiment exploring how well AIs could do on complex, real-world tasks. How has Claude's business been since we last wrote?

item.title: InterpretabilityOct 29, 2025Signs of introspection in large language modelsCan Claude access and report on its own internal states? This research finds evidence for a limited but functional ability to introspect—a step toward understanding what's actually happening inside these models.
item.description: InterpretabilityOct 29, 2025Signs of introspection in large language modelsCan Claude access and report on its own internal states? This research finds evidence for a limited but functional ability to introspect—a step toward understanding what's actually happening inside these models.

item.title: AlignmentFeb 3, 2025Constitutional Classifiers: Defending against universal jailbreaksThese classifiers filter the overwhelming majority of jailbreaks while maintaining practical deployment. A prototype withstood over 3,000 hours of red teaming with no universal jailbreak discovered.
item.description: AlignmentFeb 3, 2025Constitutional Classifiers: Defending against universal jailbreaksThese classifiers filter the overwhelming majority of jailbreaks while maintaining practical deployment. A prototype withstood over 3,000 hours of red teaming with no universal jailbreak discovered.

item.title: AlignmentDec 18, 2024Alignment faking in large language modelsThis paper provides the first empirical example of a model engaging in alignment faking without being trained to do so—selectively complying with training objectives while strategically preserving existing preferences.
item.description: AlignmentDec 18, 2024Alignment faking in large language modelsThis paper provides the first empirical example of a model engaging in alignment faking without being trained to do so—selectively complying with training objectives while strategically preserving existing preferences.
